error.signature: Circular dependency between resources: [SecurityGroup, LaunchTemplate, SecurityGroup]

verdict.summary: CloudFormation circular dependencies often arise from security groups referencing each other, or EC2 instances referencing their own security group. The error message lists the cycle but the fix requires restructuring.

dead_ends.action: Add explicit DependsOn to break the cycle
dead_ends.why_fails: DependsOn only adds dependencies, never removes them. Adding more DependsOn to a cycle makes it worse.

dead_ends.action: Merge the circular resources into one resource
dead_ends.why_fails: Merging security groups loses fine-grained access control and makes the template harder to maintain.

workarounds.action: Use SecurityGroupIngress/Egress as separate resources to break SG cycles
workarounds.how: Instead of inline ingress in SG, create separate AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress resource referencing both SGs

workarounds.action: Split the stack into nested stacks with cross-stack references
workarounds.how: Create SGs in one stack, reference them via Fn::ImportValue in another stack

workarounds.action: Use Fn::GetAtt and separate the creation from the rule attachment
workarounds.how: Create SGs with no rules -> Create rules as separate resources referencing SG IDs
